title: loading tar component manifest.json: file does not exist
body:
```
skopeo copy docker-archive:abc.tar docker://localhost:5000/abc:1.0.0
FATA[0000] initializing source docker-archive:abc.tar: loading tar component manifest.json: file does not exist
```

why?

comments.body:
Thanks for your report.

What _is_ the contents of `abc.tar`? Without a `manifest.json`, it is probably not a `docker-archive`-formatted file at all.

```
+ cat Dockerfile
FROM scratch

+ docker image build \
    --output type=tar,dest=abc.tar \
    .
[+] Building 0.0s (3/3) FINISHED                                                          docker:default
 => [internal] load build definition from Dockerfile                                                0.0s
 => => transferring dockerfile: 51B                                                                 0.0s
 => [internal] load .dockerignore                                                                   0.0s
 => => transferring context: 2B                                                                     0.0s
 => exporting to client tarball                                                                     0.0s
 => => sending tarball                                                                              0.0s

+ skopeo copy \
    docker-archive:abc.tar \
    docker://localhost:5000/abc:1.0.0
FATA[0000] initializing source docker-archive:abc.tar: loading tar component manifest.json: file does not exist 

+ tar tf abc.tar
```

```
+ docker image build --builder oci-builder --output type=oci,dest=abc.tar .
........

+ skopeo copy --dest-tls-verify=false oci-archive:abc.tar docker://localhost:5000/abc:1.0.0
Getting image source signatures
Copying config 471a1b8817 done   | 
Writing manifest to image destination
```

Solved!!!
Thanks, yes, that looks likely. I think there is another `type=` that can produce `docker-archive` outputs (and it’s not `type=tar`), but using `oci` is generally preferable anyway.
